headline: US/Israel strikes Iran; Iranian retaliation jolts global markets
analysis: Widespread military attacks and counterattacks trigger flight-to-safety. Oil surges 7.76%, equities plummet across all major indices. S&P 500 -1.1%, NASDAQ -1.5%, Stoxx 600 -1.2%. Gold rallies 3% to $5,313+. Elevated geopolitical risk premium now embedded in energy and volatility markets.

headline: S&P 500 closes at 6810, down 1.0% on Middle East escalation
analysis: US equity indices slip as geopolitical uncertainty weighs. Over one month, index down 1.55%, but still +16.42% YoY. Flight-to-safety pressures technology and discretionary sectors most.

headline: EUR/USD at 1.1737, down 0.34% today amid risk-off sentiment
analysis: Euro weakness reflects broader USD strength in risk-off environment. 12-month rally (+12.25%) now trimmed by geopolitical shock. ECB holding rates while Fed seen on pause creates rate-differential headwind for EUR.

headline: Gold surges to $5,313/oz (+3% intraday) as geopolitical safe-haven demand peaks
analysis: Middle East tensions boost gold as traditional safe-haven. JP Morgan target $6,300 EOY 2026 now more credible; Q4 2026 seen reaching $5,000+ with upside to $6,000. Central bank and investor demand remains strong at 585 tonnes/quarter.

headline: Stoxx 600 down 1.2% on Iran crisis; European indices follow global selloff
analysis: Pan-European index retreats from record highs set in early 2026. DAX, CAC 40, FTSE all under pressure. Energy and defensive sectors see intraday volatility; tech exposure to geopolitical risk clear.

headline: Bitcoin at $65,776, down 2.76% weekly; analysts debate near-term bottom vs downside risk
analysis: BTC rebounds from intraday low $63,030 but remains under weekly pressure. Polymarket shows 60% bearish consensus (<$50K target). However, Mercado Bitcoin analysis suggests BTC bottom vs gold may be nearing (April 2026). Macroeconomist Zeberg sees $110-120K upside in primary scenario (Risk-On recovery).

headline: ECB holds rates unchanged; market awaits March decision as inflation stabilizes
analysis: Governing Council keeps three key rates unchanged. Updated assessment confirms inflation trajectory to 2% target in medium term. BofA expects 25bp rate cut in March as final easing move, followed by prolonged hold. Rate guidance now anchors EUR pricing amid Fed pause.

headline: Fed holds rates at 3.50%-3.75%; March cut unlikely per JPMorgan, but 2026 cut expected
analysis: FOMC holds steady. Market assigns low odds to March cut, but strategists see at least one cut likely in 2026. Fed pausing creates widening rate differential vs ECB, supportive for USD strength and headwind for EUR.

headline: S&P 500 stages dramatic comeback, turns positive after early selloff on Iran conflict
analysis: S&P 500 recovered from early 1.2% loss to trade slightly positive by late morning. Dow Jones also trimmed 600-point loss. Traders bought the dip after U.S.-Israel strikes on Iran over weekend. Software stocks rallied (IGV ETF +1.5%).

headline: European stocks close sharply lower: STOXX 50 sinks 2.5% to 5,987, STOXX 600 down 1.5%
analysis: European markets retreat from record highs after Iran conflict escalation. Banks (Santander, BBVA, Intesa Sanpaolo -4%), consumer discretionary (Inditex, LVMH, Hermes -4%), autos (BMW, Volkswagen -5%) under pressure. Defense and energy stocks rallied.

headline: Oil surges nearly 10% to $79.40 (Brent) on Middle East supply disruption fears
analysis: Brent crude hits new 52-week high after U.S.-Israeli strikes on Iran and Iranian retaliation. Strait of Hormuz shipping fears escalate as Maersk suspends crossings. Oil stocks rally: Exxon +4%, Chevron +4%, ConocoPhillips +5%.

headline: Gold jumps to $5,408/oz as safe-haven demand spikes amid geopolitical tension
analysis: Gold rises over 2.5% as investors flee to traditional safe-haven assets. VIX volatility index surges 18% above 20. Spot gold near $5,400, futures up 3.5%. Gold's role as hedge during geopolitical stress reinforced.

headline: Bitcoin volatile at $66,195, initially pressured then recovers amid Middle East conflict
analysis: BTC initially pressured as conflict escalates, proving limited safe-haven status. Later bounced to near $70,000 (up 6% intraday). Trading around $66,195, down from yesterday's $67,412. Volume consistent as institutional investors monitor.

headline: EUR/USD technicals show sell signal with RSI at 34.974, bias remains tilted to downside
analysis: EUR/USD pair unable to gather serious traction. RSI suggests sell signal. Resistance near 1.1740; break below could test 200-day SMA. USD strengthens on risk-off flows and Fed-ECB rate differential.

headline: ISM manufacturing index at 52.4, prices index soars to 70.5 signaling inflationary pressure
analysis: US factory activity expands slightly slower but price component jumps 11.5 points to 70.5, highest in years. Backlogs up 5 points, imports up 4.9. Employment at 48.8 still contraction. Suggests persistent inflation pressures despite geopolitical uncertainty.
